element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation mechanic carried from original StarCraft; refined in Brood War with adjusted unit costs
Players must learn race-specific unit counters and strategic combinations
Special unit exclusive to campaign missions; can be merged into spellcaster units
Defensive positioning mechanic; attacks from concealment
Support unit for ground army sustainability

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Later missions when aerial threats emerge
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Counter-play to opponent air superiority; required for mission completion in specific scenarios
Shift from linear progression; requires strategic planning and tactical adaptation
AI-controlled players employ sophisticated unit combinations and timing attacks

typical_tension_triggers: Enemy army arrival at base perimeter
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Strategic pacing with increasing pressure; early game allows base establishment, mid-game introduces non-linear objectives requiring active defense, late-game features simultaneous multi-front threats and complex tactical scenarios

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory; players establish base economy and learn race fundamentals with manageable enemy pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating; non-linear mission objectives introduced, requiring strategic choice and active defense management
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty; multiple simultaneous threats, sophisticated AI tactics, complex resource management under time pressure
notable_spikes: Transition from linear to non-linear mission design (increased strategic demand)
Introduction of specialized units requiring race-specific counter-play
Late-campaign missions with multiple simultaneous objectives and aggressive AI behavior

notable_level_patterns.description: Campaign organized into three distinct episodes (Protoss, Terran, Zerg), each with unique storyline, playable faction, and strategic requirements. Each episode escalates difficulty and introduces race-specific specialized units.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Protoss Episode 1 (player as Executor), Terran Episode 2 (player as UED captain), Zerg Episode 3 (player as cerebrate)

notable_level_patterns.description: Departure from original StarCraft's linear mission design; Brood War missions offer multiple strategic approaches and decision points, requiring players to adapt tactics based on mission-specific challenges

Each race receives one unique ground unit and access to dedicated air-to-air units, creating distinct playstyle identities and forcing players to master race-specific mechanics within campaign context
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Protoss (Dark Templar merge mechanics), Terran (Combat Medic support), Zerg (Lurker burrow tactics)
